The user wants me to summarize a PDF document. I have a `summarize` skill available that can fetch URLs or convert local files (PDF/DOCX/HTML/etc.) into Markdown using `uvx markitdown`, and it can optionally summarize.

Let me first read the SKILL.md file to understand how to use this skill properly

content.text:
---
name: summarize
description: "Fetch a URL or convert a local file (PDF/DOCX/HTML/etc.) into Markdown using `uvx markitdown`, optionally it can summarize"
---

Turn “things” (URLs, PDFs, Word docs, PowerPoints, HTML pages, text files, etc.) into **Markdown** so they can be inspected/quoted/processed like normal text.

`markitdown` can fetch URLs by itself; this skill mainly wraps it to make saving + summarizing convenient.
For PDF inputs, use the `markitdown[pdf]` extra (or the wrapper below, which now does this automatically).

## When to use

Use this skill when you need to:
- pull down a web page as a document-like Markdown representation
- convert binary docs (PDF/DOCX/PPTX) into Markdown for analysis
- quickly produce a short summary of a long document before deeper work

## Quick usage

### Convert a URL or file to Markdown

Run from **this skill folder** (the agent should `cd` here first):

```bash
uvx --from 'markitdown[pdf]' markitdown <url-or-path>
```

To write Markdown to a temp file (prints the path) use the wrapper:

```bash
node to-markdown.mjs <url-or-path> --tmp
```

Tip: when summarizing, the script will **always** write the full converted Markdown to a temp `.md` file and will **always** print a final "Hint" line with the path (so you can open/inspect the full content).

Write Markdown to a specific file:

```bash
uvx --from 'markitdown[pdf]' markitdown <url-or-path> > /tmp/doc.md
```

### Convert + summarize with haiku-4-5 (pass context!)

Summaries are only useful when you provide **what you want extracted** and the **audience/purpose**.

```bash
node to-markdown.mjs <url-or-path> --summary --prompt "Summarize focusing on X, for audience Y. Extract Z."
```

Or:

```bash
node to-markdown.mjs <url-or-path> --summary --prompt "Focus on security implications and action items."
```

This will:
1) convert to Markdown via `uvx --from 'markitdown[pdf]' markitdown`
2) write the full Markdown to a temp `.md` file and print its path as a "Hint" line
3) run `pi --model claude-haiku-4-5` (no-tools, no-session) to summarize using your extra prompt

content.thinking:
This is clearly a scanned student worksheet/exercise on "Keeping Fit with Sports" - it's an English language/character education exercise for primary school children (Pr. 1 - Primary 1).

Let me provide a detailed summary of this

content.text:
Based on the scanned document, here's a summary:

## Document Summary: "Keeping Fit with Sports" Exercise (Exercise 3)

**Document Type:** Student worksheet/class exercise  
**Date:** 24th February, 2025  
**Class:** Pr. 1 (Primary 1)  
**Student Name:** Janice Wong (10)  
**Suggested Time:** 10 minutes  
**Source:** Reader - "Keeping Fit with Sports" / Ex.3

---

### **Content Overview**

This is an educational worksheet focused on **teamwork and sportsmanship**, teaching young students about what makes a good team player.

### **Section A: Team Player Actions** (Tick-the-box)
Students were asked to mark which actions represent good team players:
- ✓ **"try our best when we get a chance"** (marked with ☑)
- ✓ **"cheer our team on from the side"** (marked with ☑)
- ☐ "cry because the coach chooses another person instead"
- ✓ **"help our team win even if we are not chosen"** (marked with ☑)
- ✗ "blame our team members when the team loses"
- ✗ "be scared of the other children who are bigger than us"
- ✗ "hope that our team members can recover soon if they get hurt" (with note "get better")
- ✗ "be selfish and not share things"
- ✗ "be punctual for team practices" (with handwritten note "on time")

The student correctly identified **3 positive team behaviors**: trying their best, cheering from the sidelines, and supporting the team even when not chosen.

### **Section B: Supporting Team After a Loss**
**Question:** *"How would you cheer your team members on if your team loses?"*  
**Student's Answer:** *"I would give my team members a hug to show them my support."*

### **Section C: Team Player Attributes** (Circling Exercise)
Attributes that were **circled** as needed for a good team player:
- **Perseverance** (with note "don't give up")
- **Kindness & Empathy**
- **Commitment**

Attributes **not circled**: Filial Piety, National Identity, Self-regulation

### **Section D: Sports Mind Map**
Student brainstormed different sports in a mind map:
- **Basketball**
- **Football**
- **Baseball**
- **Karate**

---

### **Summary Assessment**
This worksheet assesses **character education and social-emotional learning** in young students. The student, Janice Wong, demonstrated:
- Good understanding of positive team behaviors
- Empathetic thinking (offering hugs after losses)
- Recognition of important character traits like perseverance, empathy, and commitment
- Basic knowledge of various sports
